name: Canada (1905–1922)
description: A red field with the Union Flag in the upper hoist canton and, centred on the fly, a composite shield bearing the arms of the nine Canadian provinces in three rows of three quarters. The shield is surmounted by a wreath and crown.
history: After Alberta and Saskatchewan were created as provinces on 1 September 1905, the Canadian Red Ensign was redrawn with a nine-quarter composite shield combining the arms of all nine provinces — an increasingly cluttered design that drew complaints. King George V granted Canada its own royal coat of arms by proclamation on 21 November 1921, replacing the composite shield with a unified design (three red maple leaves on a white base, surmounted by quarterings of England, Scotland, Ireland, and France with a fleur-de-lis), and the Red Ensign was redrawn accordingly in 1922. The 1905-pattern composite-arms ensign was thus phased out over 1921–1922.

features.symbolism: the British Red Ensign, used by Canadian merchant ships and unofficially as Canada's national flag
the Union Flag, denoting the dominion's status within the British Empire

features.role: Composite arms of the nine provinces (Ontario, Quebec, Nova Scotia, New Brunswick, Manitoba, British Columbia, Prince Edward Island, Alberta, Saskatchewan)
features.symbolism: the provinces of the Dominion of Canada
